plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-477 RP15.P08.M06.S18-RP15.P09.M05.S17 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-477 implements descriptor-only coverage that closes the RP15.P08 phase (M06 Synthetic Fixture Set tail, M07 Test And Golden Case Set, M08 Hermes Evidence Packet, M09 Claude Review Packet, M10 Closeout And Next Handoff) and opens the RP15.P09 foundation (M00 Scope Inventory, M01 Contract Draft, M02 Type And Shape Definition, M03 Primary Implementation Slice, M04 Secondary Workflow Slice, M05 Permission And Audit Binding head) as eleven descriptor-only sections over 150 planned rows with Hermes evidence (H15), Claude review packet (C15), and CP00-478 handoff, without opening analytics, Matter P&L, forecast, or WIP runtime or any other closed runtime surface.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-477 is a descriptor-only RP15 analytics-core pack (150 units, risk C, RP15.P08.M06.S18-RP15.P09.M05.S17) that closes RP15.P08 (M06 tail, M07-M10 head) and opens the RP15.P09 foundation (M00-M05 head). Independent read-only inspection via Read/Grep/Glob confirms the pack does not over-claim production readiness: manifest production_ready=false, status "implemented_pending_review", runtime_ready=false, implementation_layer "descriptor"; command-evidence production_ready=false with gate_outcome "implementation_gates_passed_post_review_closeout_pending"; the Hermes evidence packet exposes only production_ready_candidate, not a final trust claim. Unit/phase/micro/deliverable counts are internally consistent across the live plan, pack manifest, contract, registry requirements, validators, and tests (phase P08=73 + P09=77 = 150; deliverables 68 impl/9 test/38 hermes/18 claude/12 security/5 ui = 150); deviation_from_plan=false; range continuity holds at the S18 boundaries (CP476.next_subphase=S18=CP477.first_unit; CP477.next_subphase=RP15.P09.M05.S18=CP478.first_unit); and M10's 8-row section was confirmed against docs/weighted-implementation-ledger.json (exactly 8 RP15.P08.M10.S rows), so the M03/M04(20) vs M10(8) asymmetry is by ledger design, not truncation. Descriptor-only boundaries are enforced: freezeCp477Result stamps every no-write/no-real-data flag false (writes_product_state, dispatches_*_runtime, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt, creates/updates_database_rows, loads_real_fixture_data, etc.), all case-set rows carry descriptor_only:true / runtime_execution:false / raw_payload_included:false, and validateAnalyticsCoreCp477* rejects any drift. The authority boundary is preserved: descriptor authority_boundary.claude_is_final_approval=false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, and the no-write attestation sets promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false and claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false — all asserted by tests. CP178+ review hardening is intact: forbidden_review_evidence rejects failed_authentication_run, zero_byte_output, malformed_json_output, fenced_json_output, tool_call_shaped_output, missing_raw_output_reference/source_inspection_basis, claude_final_approval_claim, source_mutation_claim, and production_enterprise_trust_claim; validation-report confirms only Read/Grep/Glob tools with summary-only review prohibited (Risk C + security/review deliverables); readiness-report shows the auth preflight passed with valid JSON and non-zero stdout. Hermes patterns are used only for deterministic descriptor/receipt handling (hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product=false), never product runtime. No real client/matter/document/payments/credential data is used; service.js and validators.js import only frozen local constants with no fs/db/network. All recorded validation commands (node --check/--test, every rp*:validate, closeout-pack-plan/weighted/spec/fullplan/goal:closeout/rp00 validate, npm test, npm run validate, npm run build, git diff --check) returned exit_code 0. Claude remains a read-only independent reviewer and not the final approver; enterprise-trust closure stays a human decision outside this receipt. No P0/P1/P2 issues found; one non-blocking P3 documentation nit on the changed-file-scope classification. Pack closeout is not blocked.

findings.title: changed-file-scope dual-classifies pack-local untracked files as both implementation and unrelated-preserved
findings.details: docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md and docs/closeout-packs/cp00-477/ appear in untracked_implementation_files (lines 131-132) and simultaneously in unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (lines 137-138). The field name 'unrelated_dirty_files_preserved' reads as mutually exclusive with pack implementation files, which could confuse scope auditing. In practice the preserved list is a comprehensive safety superset of all dirty/untracked paths (the pack's own cp00-477 directory is likewise dual-listed), so no file is mis-handled and active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted is empty. This is review-scaffolding metadata only and does not touch any CP00-477 deliverable, count, boundary, or the production_ready posture.
findings.recommendation: Optionally rename/document the field (e.g., 'all_dirty_files_preserved') or exclude pack-local untracked paths from it to make the implementation-vs-unrelated distinction unambiguous for future scope audits. No code or pack change required for closeout.
findings.post_review_disposition: non_blocking_informational; appears consistent with the tool's superset-preservation pattern; acknowledged, does not block pack or goal closeout

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-477 analytics core p08 closeout p09 foundation descriptor pack
